candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue label; exact match to total revenue concept.
Not revenue; this is a cost line, so not suitable for REVT.
Also a direct top-line revenue label; appears to be the same series as Total Revenue and may be used across periods.

variables.notes: Selected revenue labels as a priority list because both are valid top-line labels and appear to represent the same reported series.

candidates.why: Direct COGS label and matches the main cost series.
Also a cost label but values are much smaller, suggesting a subcomponent rather than total COGS.
Direct COGS label and appears identical to Cost of Goods Sold; useful as fallback across periods.

candidates.row_label: Cost of Goods Sold - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing row should be excluded when a direct COGS row exists.

variables.notes: Used the direct COGS row and an alternate label as priority list; excluded balancing value and the smaller likely subcomponent line.

candidates.why: Appears to be an overhead bucket, but values equal smaller selling expense plus balancing value, suggesting subtotal rather than pure component.
Overhead component, but duplicated label structure makes it unclear whether this is the intended row versus a subcomponent.
Relevant overhead category, but duplicated exact label in the sheet creates ambiguity over which row is the intended component versus subtotal.
Relevant overhead bucket and not a total/balancing row.
Could be SG&A-related if populated, but appears empty in preview and less directly representative than other overhead rows.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. Changed. The candidate list contains duplicated labels ('Administrative expenses') and likely subtotal rows ('Selling Expenses') where exact row-level identification is ambiguous from label text alone. To avoid selecting a clear subtotal or an unresolvable duplicate, I kept the final choice minimal with the clean overhead bucket 'Other operating expenses'. Manual review is needed to determine whether the duplicated selling/administrative rows should instead be mapped as SG&A components.

candidates.why: Direct R&D expense label; even though preview is blank, the label exists and may be populated in some years.
This is D&A within R&D, not the main R&D expense line.
Balancing row should be excluded when the direct R&D label exists.

variables.notes: Selected the direct R&D line only.

candidates.why: Broad finance-cost line; acceptable fallback if no more specific interest expense is available.
Specific interest-related cost, but only a subcomponent of total interest expense.
Most direct recurring interest expense line among the candidates.
Specific interest-related cost, but likely a subcomponent rather than total interest expense.
Perfect semantic match, though preview is blank; may be used in some periods.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental lease-interest disclosure, not preferred over primary interest expense lines.

variables.notes: Provided a priority list: explicit interest expense first, then direct other interest expense, then broader financial expenses as fallback.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to the parent companys shareholders
candidates.why: Best match for book equity excluding non-controlling interests, per instructions.
Not equity; this is the balance sheet total and should not be used for BE.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to the parent companys shareholders

variables.notes: Preferred parent-shareholder equity line to avoid double counting any minority interest.
No minority interest / non-controlling interest line is present on the provided balance sheet.
